gdpr_data_access.rule: Processing employee personal data requires a lawful basis under Art. 6(1). For special-category data (health, union membership, biometrics) Art. 9 applies. Art. 88 permits Member States to adopt specific rules for employment contexts. Reviewing an employee's mailbox or device requires a documented lawful basis — typically Art. 6(1)(c) (legal obligation) or Art. 6(1)(f) (legitimate interest), with a proportionality assessment.
caveats: Employees must generally be informed of monitoring unless notice would jeopardise the investigation (Art. 5(1)(a) transparency principle)
Data minimisation: only access data strictly necessary for the investigation (Art. 5(1)(c))
Document the lawful basis and proportionality assessment before accessing personal data
National law may impose additional restrictions — check country-specific rules

whistleblower_protection.rule: Organisations with 50+ employees must establish internal reporting channels and designate a competent person to handle reports. Reports must be acknowledged within 7 days and feedback provided within 3 months. Reporters must be protected from retaliation. Identity of the reporter must be kept confidential; only disclosed with explicit consent or as required by law.
caveats: Retaliation against reporters (dismissal, demotion, harassment) is prohibited and may give rise to reversal of burden of proof in proceedings
Anonymous reports should be handled where possible under national law
Directive is implemented differently in each Member State — check national implementing act

data_retention.rule: Personal data must not be kept longer than necessary for the purpose. Case records containing personal data must have a defined retention period. Data subjects have a right to erasure once the purpose is fulfilled, subject to overriding legal obligations (e.g. limitation periods for litigation).
caveats: Retention periods for investigation records are typically aligned with applicable limitation periods (often 3–10 years depending on jurisdiction and claim type)
Define retention policy before closing a case
